item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the tone to be rather lacking, but I enjoyed the article enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of Dr.Wondertainment seeing The Foundation as the biggest fan and collector of his products.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like this SCP has gotten some fanart!

http://fav.me/dcoop4p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Self-upvoting has been allowed for a while now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You auto-upvoted it. That's bad. Shame on your cat.
But it's good anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As most, I just think it have some writing issues, but overall is great. I'll try to make some suggestions on how to improve it, though.

+ show block
– hide block

Inflated SCP-3551-1 instances are to be kept in standard humanoid containment chambers, and their SCP-3551-2 instances confiscated and stored in Anomalous Weapons Locker-52. Guards assigned to SCP-3551-1 instances are to carry standard Foundation combat knives to ensure continued cooperation. SCP-3551-1 instances are to remain under the impression that any sharp object will result in their immediate fatality, and all recovered instances are to be led to believe in the ruse as well.

Since the pistols have their own -2 designation, it seems odd to word this that way. Also, ensuring their cooperation seems like a very low priority and easy task to take so much specification on the Containment section. Maybe you could simplify to something like:

Inflated SCP-3551-1 instances are to be kept in standard humanoid containment chambers, and guards assigned to them can create any ruse to ensure cooperation if necessary. SCP-3551-2 instances are to be stored in Anomalous Weapons Locker-52.

_
_

SCP-3551 refers to a line of inflatable toy products which are designated SCP-3551-1 through 3 that are sold together. SCP-3551 come in packaging that identifies the product as "Dr. Wondertainment's Inflatable Invasion!" with a specific name of the items contained inside the packaging below it.

It is a little off tone. Maybe something like:

SCP-3551 is the common designation for three distinct inflatable products, designated SCP-3551-1 through 3. Non-inflated SCP-3551 instances are commonly found in packages with a "Dr. Wondertainment's Inflatable Invasion!" logo in the upper side, and a brief description of the items contained inside in the down side. To date, sealed packages contain one of each designation of SCP-3551.

_
_

SCP-3551 anomalous properties do not occur until it is inflated with air or helium. When fully inflated, the object will remain inflated. This is due to the plug containing the air within the item sealing on its own, and therefore can not easily be deflated.

Can be:

SCP-3551 anomalous properties do not occur until it is inflated with air or helium. When fully inflated, the plug containing the air within the item seals on its own and resists attempts to open.

_
_

SCP-3551-1 instances have the appearance of a generic "grey alien" and come in a variety of colors (see file image). Instances have a tag that brand them as "Invaders" and with a name belonging to the instance that it will identify as. Instances of SCP-3551-1 are sapient and can vocalize fluent English speech via unknown means. They have a persistent desire to "conquer the Earth" and "enslave mankind" though they are harmless and somewhat incompetent. Though instances are highly intelligent in some respects (see Incident 3551-01) despite being generally foolish and inept towards achieving their goal.

To

SCP-3551-1 instances resemble a generic "grey alien", as commonly depicted in popular culture, and come in a variety of colors. Instances have a tag that brand them as "Invaders" and with a name that the instance will identify as. Instances of SCP-3551-1 seem sapient, and can vocalize in fluent English speech via unknown means. They vocalize a persistent desire to "conquer the Earth" and "enslave mankind", though their actual actions are harmless and somewhat incompetent, apparently for comical purposes. Despite seeming generally foolish and inept towards achieving their stated goal, SCP-3551-1 instances can demonstrate advanced knowledge and technical skill, demanding constant supervision (see Incident 3551-01).

_
_

The items have external LED lights that light up when firing. Instances fire a weak red laser beam that is relatively harmless. Though…
The items have external LED lights that light up when "shooting". Instances fire a red laser through unknown means, that anomalously remains visible through its course and travels at a speed inferior to 200 Kmph. In every other respect, this laser is similar to that of common pointing devices, and as such…

_
_
Change:

The Foundation since has been having encounters with instances.

To:

Since then, the Foundation has already captured ███ instances.

_
_


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

That is mostly of it. The Incidents are good, but could use some clarification and technical details that would be of interest to the Foundation, like how they performed the hijack. The joke on using knifes to ensure cooperation when they are actually harmless to them could also be better served as an Incident or other kind of log, like an interview. Also, there are several problems with spelling and punctuation, but I'm in no position to really address them properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is good, but the writing needs some work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have something planned with these guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More of these guys will surely come. :)

item.title: I like this
item.content:encoded: My only nit to pick is that the use of the word "foolish" breaks clinical tone for me. I would expect something like "problem solving skills and strategies insufficient for basic adult responsibilities, let alone any form of conquest", perhaps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of an Series I skip (Especially with the addendums) but in a really good way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image alone put a smile on my face and tilted me into upvote territory, and I stayed there throughout the article. Nicely done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted as the core concept is great, and most of the details are fantastic, but I feel this could be improved.

An SCP-3551-1 instance was lifted by a gust of wind and, panicking, landed in Site-██. Instance was interrogated and stated that it was "studying the enemy base" from a nearby hill.

This statement is unclear. My guess is you mean it panicked, then lied about what happened. Perhaps describing it screaming for help as it was lifted would improve both tone and clarity.

I'd prefer it without the note from Wondertainment. Maybe just have the foundation note that the product is no longer produced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP still makes me smile =).

Like undergroundmonorail said, some parts could use a little rewording, but otherwise, it's a solid and humorous SCP. Nicely done. I was happy to help with the critique and advice. +1 vote for this fun SCP, I'd love to see more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ayy Lmao This gave me a laugh. I now really want to see some tales about these guys trying and failing to takeover Earth.

Take all the +1's my man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for the most part i like this one (hence my upvote) but a couple areas could use some rewording imo, most notably the transmission and the final letter. overall though it's cute and i really love the mental image of a bunch of people at a pool party fending off balloon aliens

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy. So it's been fun writing these guys. Let see how they do.

I would like to thank Professor_JD and Zyn for their quick critique.

Image credit: https://www.flickr.com/photos/50027494@N00/2255789356

Edit: These dudes came far. I'm proud. Also, TeeSherm/Site-42 on YouTube did a reading. Check it out.

https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=q0ELDNDTFFA
